senses.definition: most block-like in appearance or character, very thick or stout in form
examples: The blockiest buildings towered over the street.
She wore a blockiest sweater imaginable, wide and square-cut.

senses.definition: the quality of being block-like; thick, square, or heavy appearance or structure
examples: The blockiness of the sculpture gives it a rugged feel.
The blockiness in the font makes it hard to read at small sizes.

senses.definition: plural of blockiness; multiple instances of block-like quality
examples: The blockinesses of the old machine were evident in its rough edges.
We discussed the blockinesses in the UI at the workshop.

senses.definition: the act of obstructing or preventing movement or progress; in film/theatre, arranging actors on stage
examples: The blocking of the scene helped actors hit their marks.
People complained about the blocking of the doorway during the parade.

senses.definition: a device or action that blocks something
examples: The dam was a blocking against the flood.
A blocking of the river would alter the ecosystem.

senses.definition: resembling a block; thick, square, or heavy in appearance
examples: His blockish jaw gave him a stern look.
The blockish furniture dominated the room.

senses.definition: in a blocky, heavy, or clumsy manner
examples: The model stood blockishly in the corner.
The construction moved blockishly, as if unsure where to go next.

senses.definition: the quality of being block-like or blunt in shape or manner
examples: Her blockishness made the chair look oversized.
The blockishness of the sculpture contributed to its rugged charm.

senses.definition: plural of blockishness; multiple instances of block-like quality
examples: The blockishnesses of the fortress were evident from afar.
We noted several blockishnesses in the building's silhouette.

senses.definition: a device or material layer that blocks or holds back, or a person who blocks layers in construction
examples: The blocklayer prevented moisture from seeping in.
As a blocklayer, he stacked bricks carefully.

senses.definition: resembling a block in shape or appearance; square and solid
examples: The blocklike monument stood in the plaza.
She wore a blocklike necklace with chunky beads.

senses.definition: a line or arrangement of blocks or a line of text displayed as blocks
examples: The blockline in the game shows the next few blocks.
Read the blockline to understand the grid layout.

senses.definition: a person or thing that makes blocks or block-like structures
examples: The blockmaker produced wooden toy blocks.
This company is a famous blockmaker in the craft market.

senses.definition: the process of making blocks or block-like structures
examples: Blockmaking is a traditional craft in some regions.
They studied blockmaking techniques in the workshop.

senses.definition: a man who works with blocks (historical or fictional usage) or a laborer
examples: The blockman organized the timber blocks in the yard.
In old texts, a blockman might refer to a worker in a sawmill.

senses.definition: a period when something is blocked or unavailable; a blackout
examples: There was a power blockout last night.
They scheduled a blockout to finish the project.

senses.definition: to block out or obscure something from view or notice
examples: They block out the noise with headphones.
We blockout the schedule for a retreat.

senses.definition: a humorous or rare term; a plate or cap resembling a block
examples: The carpenter glued the blockpate to the wood.
Blockpate is not a common term in modern usage.

senses.definition: solid pieces of material (usually rectangular) used for building or playing
examples: Children stack blocks to build a tower.
The patient used wooden blocks in the therapy room.

senses.definition: a ship used to block a location or a blockade; a fictional term for a blocking vessel
examples: The fleet deployed a blockship to prevent entry.
In the story, blockships guarded the river passage.

senses.definition: plural of blockship; several ships used to form a blockade
examples: During the siege, several blockships were sunk.
The blockade included multiple blockships.

senses.definition: wood that has been cut into blocks; used as a material
examples: Blockwood was stacked in the workshop.
Blockwood pieces were used for the sculpture.

senses.definition: the work of building with blocks or brick patterns; a decorative or structural system
examples: The wall uses traditional blockwork patterns.
Blockwork is common in some rustic designs.

senses.definition: plural of blockwork; multiple constructions or works using blocks
examples: The blockworks around the plaza were restored recently.
Blockworks in the park feature brick patterns.

senses.definition: having a lot of blocks or a block-like shape; not smooth
examples: The blocky robot looked sturdy.
He wore a blocky sweater with square patterns.

senses.definition: a group of people or a formal political group; in French, means 'blocks' but used in English in certain contexts
examples: The blocs gathered to discuss policy.
Several blocs formed behind the candidate.
